summary: Matched a batch of small, mostly straightforward fighter, item, and stage functions previously left as /// # stubs. The PR filled in simple collision, physics, animation, and state-transition wrappers across Kirby copy abilities, common scope/capture behavior, Popo, items, and ground/stage modules, while adding needed header prototypes and include cleanup. The main review lesson was about using the correct fighter-vars union member by semantic owner and type: Kirby Yoshi-special animation should use fp->fv.kb.hat.jobj at the matching offset instead of borrowing the Game & Watch panicDamage member.
what_changed: Implemented ftCo_Attack100 small functions including fn_800D8378, which checks fp->item_gobj before calling ftCommon_8007E7E4(gobj, 1), and ftCo_ItemScopeEnd_Coll, which delegates to ft_800841B8(gobj, fn_800D8838).
Added ftCo_Attack100.h prototypes for fn_800D8378 and fn_800D8838.

Implemented several Kirby copy-ability helpers in ftKb_Init.c: fn_80100F60 calls ft_80089824 and ft_800892A0; ftKb_ZdSpecialN_Coll transitions via ftKb_SpecialNSk_80105E8C when ft_80082708 returns GA_Ground; ftKb_YsSpecialN1_Anim animates fp->fv.kb.hat.jobj and exits with ft_8008A2BC when animation frames end; ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Phys and ftCo_KirbyYoshiEgg_Coll branch on ground_or_air; ftKb_MsSpecialNEnd_Coll transitions on ground contact.
Added ftKb_Init.h declarations around fn_80100E0C and fn_80100F60.
Implemented ftPp_SpecialAirLw_Coll as an air collision callback wrapper around ft_80082C74(gobj, fn_80122B54), and added corresponding header declarations/comments.
Implemented grKinokoRoute_80207A98 using GET_GROUND, grAnime_801C8138, Ground_801C39C0, and Ground_801C3BB4.
Implemented grOldYoshi_8020EA64 as a simple Ground_801C2FE0 plus lb_800115F4 stage helper.
Implemented grRCruise_801FF29C as a call to grZakoGenerator_801CAE04(0).
Implemented itLizardon_UnkMotion1_Phys to apply falling physics only while the item is airborne, using x10_fall_speed and x14_fall_speed_max from ItemAttr.
Implemented it_802DE320 and it_2725_Logic11_Thrown for the Z shell item, using Item_80268E5C, it_80274C88, and Item_8026AE84.
Performed formatting and include-order cleanup in touched files, including adding ftCo_Wait.h, mpcoll.h, gr/inlines.h, lb/lb_00F9.h, and grzakogenerator.h where the new implementations required them.
smart_moves: Kept the PR focused on low-risk 'easy_funcs': short wrappers, direct state/collision transitions, and obvious physics delegations, which are good candidates for incremental matching progress.

Used existing engine helpers rather than expanding control flow manually, e.g. ft_800841B8 for scope end collision, ft_80082C74/ft_8008403C for ground-air collision callbacks, and item physics helpers for Lizardon and Z shell.

Resolved the reviewed Kirby fighter-vars issue by using fp->fv.kb.hat.jobj, an existing Kirby member at the relevant offset and with the appropriate JObj type, instead of casting or borrowing fp->fv.gw.x223C_panicDamage.
Added prototypes for newly decompiled callable symbols in headers so later files can reference the matched functions without ad hoc externs.
Preserved matching-oriented casts where needed, such as explicit enum GroundOrAir casts and HSD_GObj casts for item/stage helper calls.
decomp_lessons: For fighter-vars unions, matching offset alone is not enough. Prefer the member that belongs to the current character/system and has the right semantic type; only use a cross-character union member as a temporary clue, not as final source.

Existing same-offset struct members can avoid unnecessary struct growth. The review discussion concluded that fp->fv.kb.hat.jobj already occupied fp+0x223C and was a better fit than adding a new member or using a Game & Watch field.

Small collision functions often reduce to 'if grounded, call ground transition; otherwise call air transition' wrappers. Reuse established helper patterns like ft_80082708, ft_8008403C, and ft_80082C74.
Small physics functions often branch on ground_or_air and then delegate to shared physics helpers with data-attribute fields, as in itLizardon_UnkMotion1_Phys using ItemAttr fall-speed fields.
When turning stubs into matched functions, update companion headers in the same PR for symbols that are externally referenced or part of the module's known address table.
Include changes should track actual dependency use introduced by decompiled code; this PR added headers for ground inline access, wait/collision helpers, Zako generator calls, and lb_800115F4.
naming_conventions: Kept known address-style names for still-unknown functions, such as fn_800D8378, fn_80100F60, grOldYoshi_8020EA64, and it_802DE320.

Used subsystem prefixes consistently: ftCo_ for common fighter functions, ftKb_ for Kirby, ftPp_ for Popo, grKinokoRoute_/grOldYoshi_/grRCruise_ for ground modules, and itLizardon_/itZrshell_/it_ for item code.
Preserved motion/callback suffix conventions such as _Anim, _Phys, _Coll, _IASA, and _Thrown.
Header address comments were maintained for known addresses, e.g. /* 0D8378 */, /* 100F60 */, and /* 122B54 */; unresolved Popo entries remained commented rather than invented.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used concise wrapper implementations around known engine helpers to match small functions with minimal local variables.
Introduced explicit casts to match expected signatures and codegen, including HSD_GObj casts for item and ground helpers and enum GroundOrAir casts for ground/air comparisons.
Preserved local temporaries where they likely mattered for generated code, e.g. ItemAttr* ip_attr and Item* ip in itLizardon_UnkMotion1_Phys, and Fighter* fp or Ground* gp before helper calls.
Relied on existing inline accessors/macros such as GET_FIGHTER, GET_ITEM, GET_GROUND, and ftParts_GetBoneIndex rather than raw pointer arithmetic.
Formatted long calls and expressions without changing behavior, suggesting code style cleanup was separated from the matching logic where possible.

review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer r-burns flagged use of fp->fv.gw.x223C_panicDamage in ftKb_YsSpecialN1_Anim as semantically wrong for Kirby/Yoshi-special code and suggested using fv.kb or adding a member.
review_feedback.resolution: Author noted fp->fv.kb.hat.jobj is at the same fp+0x223C offset and works; reviewer agreed that Hat JObj sounds correct. Final diff uses HSD_JObjAnimAll(fp->fv.kb.hat.jobj).

follow_up_queries: Find other uses of cross-character fighter-vars union members where a same-offset character-specific member would be semantically better.
Audit remaining /// # easy function stubs in ftKb_Init.c around copy-ability collision and animation callbacks.
Search Popo headers for unresolved address comments around ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll and fn_80122D2C to replace placeholder comments when addresses are known.
Look for additional item physics stubs that simply delegate to it_80272860 using ItemAttr fall speed fields.
Compare stage init/update wrappers using Ground_801C2FE0, grAnime_801C8138, and grZakoGenerator_801CAE04 for reusable matching templates.
